results.question: What is the refund policy for HelixDB paid plans?
results.answer: HelixDB offers a 30-day money-back guarantee for first-time subscribers on Starter and Growth plans [chunk_1]. To request a refund, users must contact billing@helixdb.io within the first 30 days, and processed refunds are returned to the original payment method within 5–7 business days [chunk_1]. After the initial 30 days, prorated refunds for unused subscription time are not offered, though Enterprise contract terms may vary [chunk_1].

results.question: How does API key authentication work in HelixDB?
results.answer: HelixDB issues a primary API key upon registration, and users can create additional keys through the dashboard settings [chunk_1]. Authentication is performed by passing the API key in the `Authorization` header using the `Bearer` scheme [chunk_1][chunk_3]. Each key can be configured with a name, expiry date, and IP allowlist, and is assigned one of three permission scopes: Read-only, Write, or Admin [chunk_1].

results.question: What are the minimum system requirements for the HelixDB Community Edition?
results.answer: The minimum system requirements for the HelixDB Community Edition include a Linux operating system (Ubuntu 20.04+, Debian 11+, RHEL 8+, or any Linux with kernel 5.4+) and at least 4 CPU cores [chunk_1]. The system must have a minimum of 8 GB of RAM and 1 Gbps network speed [chunk_1]. Additionally, an SSD is required for storage [chunk_1].

results.question: How do I reset my HelixDB account password?
results.answer: To reset your HelixDB account password, navigate to https://app.helixdb.io/forgot-password and enter your registered email address [chunk_1]. You will receive a reset link via email that is valid for one hour; click this link to enter your new password [chunk_1]. Once the reset is complete, all existing sessions for your account will be invalidated [chunk_1].

results.question: How much does the HelixDB Growth Plan cost and what does it include?
results.answer: The HelixDB Growth Plan costs $199 per month [chunk_1]. It includes up to 50 million data points per day, 1-year data retention, unlimited dashboard workspaces, and all export formats including CSV, JSON, Parquet, and Arrow [chunk_1]. The plan also features priority email support with an 8-hour response SLA, REST API and Python/JavaScript SDKs, and webhook integrations [chunk_1].

results.question: How do I deploy HelixDB using Docker?
results.answer: To deploy HelixDB using Docker, you must first pull the latest community image with the command `docker pull helixdb/community:latest` [chunk_1]. You can then run the container using `docker run -d -p 8086:8086 -v /data/helix:/var/lib/helixdb helixdb/community:latest`, which maps port 8086 and mounts a persistent data volume [chunk_1]. This deployment method is available for the self-hosted HelixDB Community Edition [chunk_1].
